candidate_problems.text: What would count as an explanation of why arithmetic generates the zero-geometry of zeta, rather than merely another technique for controlling zeros within the inherited analytic framework?
Are there alternative representations of prime structure that preserve the power of the zeta translation while making the arithmetic-analytic bridge itself more intelligible?
Could RH be proved entirely within existing analytic machinery without any deeper explanatory bridge, and if so would that undermine the claim that its hardness stems from representational mismatch?
